message:
v0.3.12: Double min_observations to 60 and fix scanner candidate count

Version bump 0.3.11 → 0.3.12.

guardrails.rs + config.rs:
- Increase guardrail_min_observations default from 30 to 60. The
  original threshold was too low — on machines with bursty workloads,
  30 observations can complete within a single burst window, causing
  the guard to prematurely reach PASS based on insufficient steady-state
  data. 60 observations span at least 5 minutes at the default 5-second
  tick interval, providing a more representative calibration window.

loop_main.rs:
- Fix scanner candidate count to include priority pre-scan candidates.
  Previously, candidates_found was declared after the priority pre-scan
  and only counted general walker hits. Now initialized before the
  priority pass and incremented when high-value candidates are
  dispatched, so the summary report reflects the true total.

decision_plane_tests.rs:
- Update warmup_to_mode to feed 65 good observations (was 35) to match
  the new min_observations=60 default.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1,6 +1,6 @@
 [package]
 name = "storage_ballast_helper"
-version = "0.3.11"
+version = "0.3.12"
 edition = "2024"
 license = "MIT"
 description = "System service that prevents disk-full scenarios from coding agent swarms via ballast files, artifact scanning, and special location monitoring."
